title: SQL CRUD Operations Quiz
description: Test your fundamental knowledge about the four core operations of persistent storage in SQL.

questions.question: What does the 'CRUD' acronym stand for?
Which SQL statement is used to add new rows of data to a table?
What's the primary risk of not using a 'WHERE' clause with an UPDATE or DELETE statement?
options: You could accidentally update or delete every single row in the table, potentially losing all data.
It's a requirement of all CSS frameworks.
It automatically generates a backup of the data.

questions.question: Which SQL clause is used to group rows that have the same values into summary rows (usually used with aggregate functions)?
What is a 'Soft Delete' in the context of database management?
options: Deleting the table instead of a single row.
A way to encrypt data before deleting it.
Instead of actually removing a row, you set a 'deleted_at' timestamp or an 'is_deleted' flag to hide it.
